title: Cleome inornata
taxonomy: Cleome inornata is a species of flowering plant in the family Cleomaceae. It belongs to the genus Cleome, which includes over 170 species of annuals and perennials. Cleome inornata is native to the southwestern United States, specifically in Arizona, California, and New Mexico. The plant is also known as the 'Mexican Poppy' or 'Yellow Jarina' due to its vibrant yellow flowers. The classification of Cleome inornata is further supported by its unique characteristics and growth habits, making it a notable member of the Cleomaceae family.
uses: Cleome inornata is a plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa. It is believed to have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In traditional medicine, Cleome inornata is often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices to administer its medicinal properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Cleome inornata for medicinal use.
benefits: Cleome inornata has been traditionally used in folk medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits.
